arms_mandatory_baselines: kernel-v0 + molecules-v0 + lexical-wn31 committed records (pinned static corpus, bulk >= 10^4)

dependent_vars.definition: records breaching the 32-clause grammar cap OR >1.5x clause growth under the one-level inline-only rendering vs the reference/apply rendering (union, per record; both accounting choices understate inline pressure — biased against the kill)
dependent_vars.name: n_breached_either

dependent_vars.definition: bulk record set: kernel-v0 + molecules-v0 + lexical-wn31 (>= 10^4)
dependent_vars.name: n_records_bulk

endpoints.test: deterministic bulk-projection count vs the pre-registered 10% bound (NF6); count-based fork criterion, carries no statistical test (P1 common rules)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS2–HS8 (verbatim): Measured range: R0 — no host model. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: Model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism); the relevant axis is KERNEL size: verdicts on 54–10³ records re-checked at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring (G6/G7 re-run as regression). Literature anchor + licensing assumption: Formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale; only corpus composition shifts them.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS7 statement + kill (verbatim): '(HS7) inlining relational content forces cap violations or >1.5× clause growth on >10% of records at bulk scale ⇒ apply-clauses (kot-ast/2) win; else defer.' Kill (P1 §8): 'cap violations/1.5× growth >10% ⇒ kot-ast/2.' Kill criteria are the numeric bounds from NF6, verbatim. FAIL here means the fork selects kot-ast/2 (a design selection, not a programme kill).

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py

title: G7 — apply-clause cap/growth bulk projection over the committed corpora (HS7 / NF6; deterministic count, no test; pinned one-level inline projection, biased against the kill — correction c-g7-1)
